title: Request Context, Deadlines, Cancellation, and Cleanup · Program Defense
summary: A production-readiness review compares near-miss decisions for request context, deadlines, cancellation, and cleanup and requires the smallest safe deadline budget and cancellation propagation trace with changed-request evidence. Planned artifact: Record independent decisions and explanations for deadline budget and cancellation propagation trace. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Trace when an incoming request context is canceled and pass it through every request-scoped operation without storing it in long-lived structs.
Allocate caller deadline across validation, database, outbound dependency, response, and cleanup reserves instead of stacking independent full timeouts.
Distinguish client cancellation, server deadline, dependency timeout, shutdown, overload, and internal failure for response and observability decisions.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Go server Diagnose the hidden failure · Quiz Cleanup Compensation
steps.instruction:
quiz case — A quiz team handles case 5075 in a partner API with an outdated generated client. Build deterministic Go evidence to derive a dependency budget while reserving time for cancellation cleanup; then saturate one admission budget while another tenant remains serviceable. Browser code models decisions only. Listener, TLS, database, proxy, race, load, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Reserve cleanup time, release rows and transactions, compensate partial external effects, and verify no work outlives its intended authority.

The visible result looks correct, but its invariant or value contract is unverified. What must happen next?

Evidence target: Go server Diagnose the hidden failure · Quiz Cleanup Compensation.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for reserve cleanup time, release rows and transactions, compensate partial external effects, and verify no work outlives its intended authority. For Go server Diagnose the hidden failure · Quiz Cleanup Compensation, keep Cleanup Compensation connected to executable HTTP client decision evidence.

steps.title: Go server Defend one difficult decision · Quiz Cleanup Compensation
steps.instruction:

Build deterministic Go evidence to derive a dependency budget while reserving time for cancellation cleanup; then replay the same operation key with different content and reject the mismatch. Browser code models decisions only. Listener, TLS, database, proxy, race, load, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Reserve cleanup time, release rows and transactions, compensate partial external effects, and verify no work outlives its intended authority.
